issues.title: Spellcheck code uses context across an asynchronous gap
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

I enabled spellcheck for `TextFormField`:
```
final spellCheck = kIsWeb
        ? null
        : SpellCheckConfiguration(
            spellCheckService: DefaultSpellCheckService(),
          );
```
and published to GooglePlay to get a pre-launch report

### Expected results

No exceptions are thrown

### Actual results

Crashlytics recorded "crashes" with the following stacktrace:
```
Fatal Exception: io.flutter.plugins.firebase.crashlytics.FlutterError: Null check operator used on a null value. Error thrown .
       at State.context(framework.dart:958)
       at EditableTextState.buildTextSpan(editable_text.dart:5071)
       at EditableTextState._performSpellCheck(editable_text.dart:3843)
```
Quick review of the code lead me to the
```
final List<SuggestionSpan>? suggestions = await
        _spellCheckConfiguration
          .spellCheckService!
            .fetchSpellCheckSuggestions(localeForSpellChecking!, text);

      if (suggestions == null) {
        // The request to fetch spell check suggestions was canceled due to ongoing request.
        return;
      }

      spellCheckResults = SpellCheckResults(text, suggestions);
      renderEditable.text = buildTextSpan();
```

### Code sample

-

### Screenshots or Video

_No response_

### Logs

_No response_

### Flutter Doctor output

<details open><summary>Doctor output</summary>

Thanks for sharing this! I’m getting only script errors running this and following the steps.
Log

``` ════════ Exception caught by widgets ═══════════════════════════════════════════ The following assertion was thrown while performing spell check: This widget has been unmounted, so the State no longer has a context (and should be considered defunct). Consider canceling any active work during "dispose" or using the "mounted" getter to determine if the State is still active. When the exception was thrown, this was the stack #0 State.context. framework.dart:951 #1 State.context framework.dart:957 #2 EditableTextState.buildTextSpan editable_text.dart:5363 #3 EditableTextState._performSpellCheck editable_text.dart:4116 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ ```

Can you help with a screen recording and full logs you get?

Yes, the exception you are showing is what I'm getting in Crashlytics every time the automated tests are run in Google Play. And it's exactly what this lint rule is about: [use_build_context_synchronously](https://dart.dev/tools/linter-rules/use_build_context_synchronously)

Also, I started getting a similar but new stack trace that is, likely, also caused by concurrency issue.
```
Fatal Exception: io.flutter.plugins.firebase.crashlytics.FlutterError: Null check operator used on a null value. Error thrown .
       at TextPainter.computeLineMetrics(text_painter.dart:1599)

       at BasicMessageChannel.setMessageHandler.<fn>(platform_channel.dart:235)
       at _DefaultBinaryMessenger.setMessageHandler.<fn>(binding.dart:603)
```
Both exceptions seem to be triggered only during Google Play automated tests. And don't seem to cause any UI issues.
Even if they are harmless, how can I filter them out?

Reproducible using the code sample in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/144165#issuecomment-1966124770

@dvoloshyn, please file another issue for the exception in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/144165#issuecomment-2001879574

Here's a workaround, wrap your drawer with a column+sized-box:
```dart
Column(
  children: [
    Expanded(child: Drawer()),
    SizedBox(height: MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom),
  ],
```

What I done is create a **Scaffold** widget as Drawer's child and set **resizeToAvoidBottomInset** to true. This worked for me.

Reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.7, Textfield gets hidden behind a keyboard in a drawer while it is focused properly in the body of the Scaffold.

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
```

issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce 1. Change your locale to Japanese 2. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample 3. Tap text field **Expected results:** for example, it should be `すべてを選択` like iOS native todo app **Actual results:** for example, it shows `すべて選択` **native vs flutter** | native(expected) | flutter(actual) | correct | ----|----|---- | すべてを選択 | すべて選択 | × | | カット | 切り取り | × | | コピー | コピー | ○ | | ペースト | 貼り付け | × |

cc: @justinmc
@cbracken 🇯🇵

Looks to me like we're using the Android (Material?) wording for these options rather than the iOS wording. The options are comprehensible but not what an iOS user expects. See Android screenshots below: Default Android menu for a selection looks like: 翻訳　切り取り　コピー　取り付け (Translate, Cut, Copy, Paste) Default Android menu when tapping the cursor: 取り付け　すべて選択 (Paste, Select All) @goderbauer I believe these are being automatically generated through Translation Console, is that correct? From a fidelity point of view, we should be using the same translation as the platform rather than the default 'Material' translations (if that's where we're getting them). Do we have a source for translations that match up with those on iOS? This is likely a much more generic issue that applies to other languages where the wording used for Cut, Copy, Paste differs between platforms. We'll have similar issues for the desktop platforms -- e.g. a quick look at Windows and Linux (GTK) in Japanese shows the same wording as Android, whereas macOS uses the same wording as iOS, but I'd be a bit sceptical that the [Android, Windows, Linux] vs [iOS, macOS] pattern holds up across all languages. /cc @jmagman FYI

It seems to be generated for cupertino and material instead of by platform.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9cf042ec746629301c9c1c7b6e220b02eddec4ec/packages/flutter_localizations/lib/src/l10n/generated_material_localizations.dart#L19026
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9cf042ec746629301c9c1c7b6e220b02eddec4ec/packages/flutter_localizations/lib/src/l10n/generated_cupertino_localizations.dart#L5873
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9cf042ec746629301c9c1c7b6e220b02eddec4ec/packages/flutter_localizations/lib/src/l10n/cupertino_ja.arb#L21

Since we build different UI for the toolbar based on the platform [1] it should be enough to just fix the cupertino translations to match user's expectations on iOS. The Cupertino toolbar (used on iOS) already uses these Cupertino translations [2].

> I believe these are being automatically generated through Translation Console, is that correct?

Not sure if it is fully automated, I think there are still people doing (some of) the actual translating. Either way, there is a way to give feedback for a translation and request a different one. @HansMuller may know how to do that.

---
[1] https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/dffddf00a22e77122d67f4503efef16a46c18ec2/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L1152-L1156
[2] https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/64a0c19652a63292f7587a52f5f24656291ffd35/packages/flutter/lib/src/cupertino/text_selection.dart#L143

Talked to @HansMuller and he thinks my hunch above that we're getting these from Translation Console is likely right.

My guess is we almost certainly have a source for iOS/macOS translations for these internally, since they'd be used in existing iOS apps -- even if those use UITextField, we almost certainly support cutting/pasting things other than text and feature those in the UI.

> Since we build different UI for the toolbar based on the platform [1] it should be enough to just fix the cupertino translations to match user's expectations on iOS.

This should fix the problem for iOS and macOS, and would be great to do.

Echoing a point @cbracken made above (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/105028#issuecomment-1144295524), that will probably still leave this problem open on Windows and Linux (though it happens not to for Japanese). I think that can be a separate issue, though.

Looking at this today, the underlying string is shared in translation console. As noted above, what we really need is a string for Apple platforms (which we'd use on both Material and Cupertino) and a string for Windows/Linux/Android (which _currently_ happen to have the same translation for the above strings at least).

issues.title: Can't bring custom selection into view from Scrollable when showing keyboard
comments: That is a nasty work arround that I figured out indeed in the last hour. But that is not something I want to be using for production.

Bumping this. Still broken in production-ensure visible isn't useful here because it ensures instantaneously. I just want to replicate the behavior of `EditableText`:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/fc8ecbced784a348f0c869a31b81c7a2d030fabe/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3708

When tapping on a `TextField`, it automatically scrolls to stay in view after the keyboard is selected. This is the behavior I'd expect from `ensureVisible`. I'd suggest either:
1. Exposing a second method that provides a public version of `EditableText`'s internal logic
or
2. Updating `ensureVisible` such that it replicated `EditableText`'s behavior.

Either way, as is, it's very confusing/messy attempting to replicate `TextField`'s behavior when using a non-`TextField` widget.

issues.title: Windows app crashes when pressing "Insert" key.
issues.body:
When I run a Windows app, and press "Insert" using the master channel, it crashes the app.

When it crashes, in the (Dart) exception, it gives the key event as:

```
RawKeyDownEvent#a2ba8(logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#000ff(keyId: "0x16000000ff", keyLabel: "",
debugName: "Key with ID 0x016000000ff"), physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#70053(usbHidUsage:
"0x00070053", debugName: "Num Lock")) and its data: RawKeyEventDataWindows#f7e83(keyCode: 255,
scanCode: 57413, characterCodePoint: 0, modifiers: 0)
```

Which is weird to me, since it isn't the NumLock key, it's the Insert key.

The keycode for Insert should be 0x2d, not 0xff.

It's might be crashing because it looks at "NumLock" and thinks it needs to synchronize modifiers, but no modifier flags are set (because it's not a modifier).

Reproduction steps:

1) `flutter create --platform=windows foo`
2) `cd foo`
2) `flutter run -d windows`
2) Press "Insert" key.
3) Crash.

I was able to repro this with the b865e23 master build.

cc @dkwingsmt
comments: It doesn't appear to matter what the application is: just pressing Insert in a Windows app crashes, even if a text field isn't focused.
I still can't trigger it somehow... Can you investigate it for me? Specifically, can you try lifting the limitation in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/93897?

It looks like I can't reproduce this when using remote desktop and a macOS laptop as  the host (it's hard to make an "Insert" key press there anyhow, but I tried a virtual keyboard, along with Fn-Ctrl-Enter).

I've done some testing, but I'm not done yet: I'd like to find out the root cause.  In any case, it seems like some fuzzing might not be a bad idea: we do have some weird cases where crashes are occuring, and I would like to feel comfortable that pressing a key can't crash a (non-debug) app.

We are currently trying to contact Samsung for diagnosing the issue and to set up tests to prevent similar regressions in the future.

@LongCatIsLooong Just wondering if you ever heard anything back from Samsung on this – running into it with a custom text editor in another app, but I did a little investigation with the example here. Interestingly, on `master`, with the example as-is, the delete button does not work. However, if I move the text input inside the `MaterialApp`, it works as expected.

I'll probably take a deeper look at this, but just wanted to see if you have any thoughts. I think this is basically the last issue preventing us from upgrading to Flutter 2.8.1

For what it's worth, I did a bisect and landed on this 136da59e6b47eac6f2c0cdfaf376bd755b46690a as the first bad commit – it has something to do with text editing actions, so I suppose that could make sense, though I was expecting something in the engine.
It looks like in my case, I needed to implement `TextEditingActionTarget` on my custom EditableTextState (it was already inside a `MaterialApp`)

As for solving the actual issue at hand here, would it make sense to change `EditableText` so that it will provide the `DefaultTextEditingShortcuts` and `DefaultTextEditingActions` if it doesn't have them in it's ancestor tree?

> I did find a workaround for @ueman 's example – wrapping the `FeedbackBottomSheet` with `DefaultTextEditingShortcuts` and `DefaultTextEditingActions` restores the backspace button

That's awesome, thank you very much @fbcouch

I checked into this a bit more because I was upgrading to flutter 2.10. It looks like `DefaultTextEditingActions` has been removed because `EditableText` now provides default `Actions`. So perhaps the answer is for it to provide a similar "overridable" version of `Shortcuts` as well? At any rate, you only need to wrap your `FeedbackBottomSheet` in `DefaultTextEditingShortcuts` now

@fbcouch unfortunately I haven't heard back from Samsung yet. If the IME sends backspace as a hardware keyboard event yeah you'll have to add a shortcut and implement the action to handle the backspace key.
I have tried the solution provided by @fbcouch and unfortunately it didn't work. Is there any workaround for this issue?

> @fbcouch unfortunately I haven't heard back from Samsung yet. If the IME sends backspace as a hardware keyboard event yeah you'll have to add a shortcut and implement the action to handle the backspace key.

Could you please provide code example to implement this solution? Thanks for the clarification.

@cbracken

This is surprising - this was fixed in https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/39460. I'll see if I can repro it locally.

@darshankawar you mentioned above:
> I verified this issue using latest stable and master version and noticed that it is replicable only on stable and beta, but works as expected on latest master. Upon typing in the textfield, the touch bar doesn't show anything as expected.

But below:
> Thanks for the update. Using steps to replicate above, I was able to verify it as reported.

What was the difference between those two attempts to verify?

Ah I see - I'm guessing due to the show step which I suppose removes and re-sets the `obscureText` setting. My guess (without having tested yet) is that just changing obscureText is perhaps not triggering the framework to send an update to the embedder, and this is likely an issue on all platforms. I'll see what can be done to fix this.

@AdamVe In the meantime, if this is a password field, please set your [TextField.autofillHints](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/autofillHints.html) to include [AutofillType.password](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/AutofillHints/password-constant.html), which will ensure that autocomplete is disabled for the field at all times, which I think is what you want for a password field. You can pass this via the constructor.

I've updated the issue description, sample code, and repro steps from @AdamVe's [later comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/122300#issuecomment-1463449686), and I've retitled it to the more general issue.

Alright I did a bit of looking at this and I believe this is indeed an issue for all platforms, and is not Mac-specific. A TextField's `obscureText` property is part of the [TextInputConfiguration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextInputConfiguration/TextInputConfiguration.html) object, which is passed from the framework to the embedder during as part of the `TextInput.setClient` channel method, which we fire when focus is moved to the textfield.

I was surprised to discover that we actually do have a [TextInput.updateConfig](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/1306d7f1588c9de4c8534b90308494c3630a8c5a/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L2248-L2254) call which today is only implemented for Flutter Web. 

I banged up a quick prototype that checks for a change in `obscureText` in [EditableText.didUpdateWidget](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/1306d7f1588c9de4c8534b90308494c3630a8c5a/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L2677), and if present, fires a `TextInput.updateConfig` across the channel, where I parse it out and apply the updates on in the macOS embedder and it does successfully pick up the new obscureText setting and disable autocomplete.

All that said, if we do apply a fix here, I think the fix should be more general than obscureText (perhaps any change to the configuration?) and then fire `TextInput.updateConfig`, and implement appropriate handling for each platform. I'm happy to make the framework and macOS changes, but I'd really like to hear thoughts from @goderbauer and @justinmc on this specifically on which conditions we'd fire this -- e.g. is changing the text editing delta setting mid-edit a good idea?

@AdamVe for your specific case, the right thing to do is to mark this as a password field as I've [described above](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/122300#issuecomment-1467119782), which will fix your issue today (on `master` branch) with no changes required on the Flutter side.

Thank you @cbracken! I confirm that with `AutofillHints.password` the touch bar does not show any clear text in Flutter, Channel beta, 3.8.0-10.1.pre, on macOS 13.2.1 22D68 darwin-arm64 🎉 That is great news!!

Are there any plans for cherrypicking this into stable, or should I wait for 3.9?

@cbracken The workaround works good. I just stumbled upon following: not all fields which we obscure are meant to be passwords. Using `AutofillHints.password` will activate Autofill Service on Android for these fields which might not be ideal.

I add the hint for non-Android platforms only to avoid Autofill Service activation.

> Are there any plans for cherrypicking this into stable, or should I wait for 3.9?

There are no current plans to cherry-pick to stable.

> Using `AutofillHints.password` will activate Autofill Service on Android for these fields

Just to check - do you mean it activates regular autocomplete, or password autocomplete (i.e. password manager integration)? If the latter, that's intentional, though not yet implemented across all platforms. If the former, it's definitely a bug and we should get it fixed -- if so would you find filing a bug with what you saw? (I initially filed https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/122613 but had assumed the latter scenario, so better to be sure).

We do have a separate input type called [visiblePassword](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextInputType/visiblePassword-constant.html) which may do what you want, but I don't know that we actually use it to disable autocomplete when that input type is present; it appears to be android-specific at the moment.

Thanks for your comments, I will verify the type of autocomplete (regular vs password manager) and fill a bug if appropriate. I will also check the visible password behaviour - might be exactly what we need. This issue can be closed, have a great day!

From the framework's point of view, I think it would be a good thing to sync all of TextInputConfiguration with the engine when any of the fields change in the framework.  I think that the only reason we haven't run into this as a problem before is that these settings usually don't change while the field has focus.  The example in this issue (tapping a suffix button) used to steal focus from the input until fairly recently (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/107262, if I'm not mistaken).

Even for something tricky like enableDeltaModel, I think it's up to the programmer to decide if they want to change it while the connection is active or not, even if it seems dangerous to most applications in practice.  CC @Renzo-Olivares in case you have opinions.

What I don't know is how our embedders and platforms will handle having these values change on them.  Maybe there are some parameters that won't take effect until restarting the connection due to how the platform handles them.

So I say, pass the whole TextInputConfiguration and see if it breaks anything!  As long as it doesn't seem overwhelming or too risky.  Let me know if I can help with anything there.

SGTM. Will wait for @Renzo-Olivares's opinion but if he's good with this, I've got an implementation for macOS engine ready to go, and it seems to behave well when I wired it up to flip obscuretext/autocomplete on the fly.

@cbracken sorry for the late response I missed this. I don't see why someone would flip `enableDeltaModel` on and off but I think that would be up to the developer to handle the side-effects. This sounds like a reasonable change to me rather than updating only some of the `TextInputConfiguration`. Maybe a warning in the docs for some of the more tricky configuration options if warranted.
Another issue (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/122139) seems like it would also be fixed if we synced the obscureText value with the engine while the connection was still open.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

Now imagine how many people are using MediaQuery in their code on latest release without realising that their widgets are being rebuilt :D

[StatelessWidget.build](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/StatelessWidget/build.html)

> The framework calls this method when this widget is inserted into the tree in a given [BuildContext](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/BuildContext-class.html) and when the dependencies of this widget change (e.g., an [InheritedWidget](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/InheritedWidget-class.html) referenced by this widget changes). This method can potentially be called in every frame and should not have any side effects beyond building a widget.

Since `MediaQuery` has changed, this is the expected behavior. For instance, if you change the text scale factor, your dependent widgets will rebuild. The same goes for `Theme.of`.

The build method returns widgets, which are blueprints. The framework is smart enough to determine what has changed, but you can assist it if you are returning the same instance, for instance, by using the `const` keyword, so `build` method won't do too many things.

> [StatelessWidget.build](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/StatelessWidget/build.html)

This method can potentially be called in every frame and should not have any side effects beyond building a widget.

> 
> The build method returns widgets, which are blueprints. The framework is smart enough to determine what has changed, but you can assist it if you are returning the same instance, for instance, by using the `const` keyword, so `build` method won't do too many things.

So you are telling me that behavior displayed in video below is okay?...

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/54116985/363e3bfc-39a9-4e7d-af57-eac289182877

> > So you are telling me that behavior displayed in video below is okay?...
> 
> Yes that is okay.
> 
> However there is still room for optimise your code.
> 
> Change this:
> 
> ```dart
> Column(
>   children: [
>     Container(
>       height: MediaQuery.of(context).size.height / 2,
>       width: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width / 2,
>       color: Colors.red,
>     ),
>     Expanded(
>       child: ListView(
>         children: List.generate(100000, (index) => Text('index: $index')),
>       ),
>     )
>   ],
> );
> ```
> 
> To:
> 
> ```dart
> return Column(
>   children: [
>     const RedContainer(),
>     Expanded(
>       child: ListView.builder(
>         itemBuilder: (context, index) => Text('index: $index'),
>       ),
>     )
>   ],
> );
> 
> class RedContainer extends StatelessWidget {
>   const RedContainer({super.key});
> 
>   @override
>   Widget build(BuildContext context) {
>     return Container(
>       height: MediaQuery.of(context).size.height / 2,
>       width: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width / 2,
>       color: Colors.red,
>     );
>   }
> }
> ```

Yeah, I know about the .builder constructor. That's not about optimizing the code. It's about parent screen being rebuilt while I'm currently on a second screen, which is strange. The reason for List.generate(100000, (index) => Text('index: $index')) is to show you that we are not hitting 60fps in case we are using MediaQuery because the screen is rebuilt on every frame... Was it always like this in flutter or it was caused by one of the PRs ([this one](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/114459) or [that one](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/118004), specifically)?
Aha, understood, thanks for clarification . Well, I guess in my case I should just go for LayoutBuilder instead of MediaQuery since I don’t want those rebuilds. What do you think?

> Aha, understood, thanks for clarification . Well, I guess in my case I should just go for LayoutBuilder instead of MediaQuery since I don’t want those rebuilds. What do you think?

The `MediaQuery` always returns the view size, i.e., the screen size. Some widgets like to provide their own, but they usually copy the original one and add some padding, although this is rare. On the other hand, `LayoutBuilder` returns constraints. For instance, if your `LayoutBuilder` is deep down in the widget tree, it will have different constraints based on its position in the tree. Sometimes, you may get `double.infinity` `height`/`width` if the constraint is a loose one. Therefore, you should choose the one that best fits your job.

You can still use `MediaQuery`. However, use `MediaQuery.sizeOf(context)` instead of `MediaQuery.of(context).size`, since when the keyboard opens, the size will be unaffected. This means your first screen won't call the `build` method.

issues.title: `FocusNode.nextFocus()` doesn't seem to focus the next node
comments:
Thank you@huycozy. From your example (when you use the text buttons), when you click on the `skip_next` icon button, it moves the focus to the node which is after the one that is already focused. Is it the expected behavior?

What I was trying to do with `focusNodeButton.nextFocus();` was to move the focus to the node that is after the `focusNodeButton`, not the node that is after the current focused one. 
So in your example, I was hoping to always focus the text button `4` after clicking on the button. 

How could I implement what I'm trying to do?

>So in your example, I was hoping to always focus the text button 4 after clicking on the button.

Do you mean it should skip focusing on `skip_next` button when clicking on it?

> Do you mean it should skip focusing on `skip_next` button when clicking on it?

Focusing the button during/after the click doesn't really matter to me. What I want is that after I click on this button (that you could replace with a text button with the label "focus field after this button"), I want the field following the button to be focused

>I want the field following the button to be focused

It currently does with TextButton. That's the reason why I marked this issue related to TextField as your initial example.

> It currently does with TextButton. That's the reason why I marked this issue related to TextField as your initial example.

In the video you shared with the text button:
1. Initially, the focus is on the button `3`
2. You click on the `skip_next` button -> The focus moved to the button `skip_next`
3. You click on the `skip_next` button -> The focus moved to the button `4`
4. You click on the `skip_next` button -> The focus moved to the button `5`

What I am trying to do:

1. Initially, the focus is on the button `3`
2. You click on the `skip_next` button -> The focus moves to the button `4` (the button after the `skip_next` button)
3. You click on the `skip_next` button -> The focus moves to the button `4` (the button after the `skip_next` button) (so there is no change)
4. You click on the `skip_next` button again -> The focus moves to the button `4` (the button after the `skip_next` button) (so there is still no change)
Buttons don't request focus when they are clicked, so the "next" focus is always the focus node that is next in the focus traversal order. Perhaps this is why it doesn't do what you are expecting?

@gspencergoog the fact that it is a button doesn't really matter.

I'm calling `focusNodeButton.nextFocus();` so I'm expecting the focus to go the node that is after `focusNodeButton` (The text field 4 in the text field example, or text button 4 in the text button example).

What I'm not trying to do:
Focus the next node after the currently focused one 

What I'm trying to do:
Focus the next node after the `FocusNode focusNodeButton` (which can be focused already or not)

Consider replacing `Container` with `ColoredBox`. What happened here is since the color goes from `null` to a non-null color, the `Container` widget changes the tree structure (and you did not give TextFied a global key), so the framework removed the old `TextFied` and inflated a new `TextField`. 

`EditableText` needs to handle the case where it's given a focused focusNode in `initState`.

issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. run the code below 
2. run flutter build web --release
3. open the website on mobile.

I also hosted the code below and you can access via this link https://timezone-dot-dev-public-383006.ts.r.appspot.com/#/. 

I do not get this issue for all the mobile web browsers. I tried with iphone 13 safari browser as well as multiple Samsung  phones and they are fine (the keyboard obscures the text field which is intended). However, I tested with pixel 5 phone (Android Version 13) and got this issue . 

### Expected results

From the flutter documentation, when resizeToAvoidBottomInset i set to false, scaffold should not be resized and the text field should be obscured. 

### Actual results

Text field is pushed to above keyboard. 

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';


void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple),
        useMaterial3: true,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Timezone'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});
  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  TextEditingController controller = TextEditingController();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false,
      appBar: AppBar(
        backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.inversePrimary,
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Padding(
        padding: const EdgeInsets.all(20.0),
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.end,
          children: [
            Padding(
              padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 20),
              child: TextField(
                  controller: controller,
                  cursorColor: Colors.pink,
                  style: const TextStyle(
                  color: Colors.pink
              )),
            ),
          ]
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

So it appears to be only reproducible on physical Android devices + chrome? I'll try to repro this on a Pixel and investigate further.

I've reproduced this via remote debugging an android device (Pixel 6, Android 13, Chrome) - I've tried using the same trick from the `IOSTextEditingStrategy` to delay placement of the text field, but it doesn't change anything. - I've tested this with both `true` and `false` for `resizeToAvoidBottomInset` and there's a very subtle difference. When `true`, the entire scaffold resizes and the headers remains static. When `false` (when the bug is occurring), the field gets pushed up and the header slightly scrolls out of view. - I'm not sure this is a text field issue as much as it's a scaffold resize issue and a bug with the `resizeToAvoidBottomInset` property. Adding a `framework` label to see if the framework team has any insights on this. Additional notes: - When the property is `true`, `minInsets.bottom` has an actual value - When the property is `false`, `minInsets.bottom` remains 0, even though the field is still getting pushed up. - I think this indicates that the scaffold is inadvertently resizing and there's a bug with `resizeToAvoidBottomInset` Screenshots (these were taken during remote debugging, so they won't show the virtual keyboard - but assume the keyboard is there): When the property is `true`, it behaves as expected. When the property is `false`, the field still gets pushed up, but also notice that the header gets scrolled out of the way. The entire scaffold looks like it's resizing. It also seems like the entire app gets pushed out of view.

## Steps to Reproduce

Create a `TextField` that does not allow multi-line input (i.e. don't set `maxLines: null`).

Then set the text of the `TextField` to a string that contains a newline (so that part of the text will not be visible in the text widget), using `setText` (shown below).

This method tries to set the caret position to a position past the end of the visible input in this case, which triggers the exception.

```dart
  final controller = TextEditingController();

  setText(String text) {
    controller.text = text;
    // Move cursor to end of text
    controller.selection = TextSelection.fromPosition(
      TextPosition(offset: controller.text.length),
    );
  }
```

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Probably the `TextPosition` should be set to the closest visible position, rather than failing an assertion if the requested position is not visible.
